steps.action: The gumboot dance (or Isicathulo) is a South Afr with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The gumboot dance (or Isicathulo) is a South Afr with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: The boots may be embellished with bells, so that
steps.text: The boots may be embellished with bells, so that they ring as the dancers stamp on the ground.

steps.action: Initially a codified tap used by black miners de
steps.text: Initially a codified tap used by black miners deprived of conversation, gumboot dancing today is one of the most expressive South African dance genres.

steps.action: Many of the steps and routines are parodies of t
steps.text: Many of the steps and routines are parodies of the officers and guards who controlled the mines and barracks of South African gold miners.

steps.action: Like other forms of African dance, Gumboot utili
steps.text: Like other forms of African dance, Gumboot utilizes the concepts of polyrhythm and total body articulation, drawing from the cultural dances of the African workers that worked the mines.

steps.action: The album Graceland by the American pop singer P
steps.text: The album Graceland by the American pop singer Paul Simon has a song titled "Gumboots", which is performed in the style of South African township jive (mbaqanga) and contains performances by members of the Boyoyo Boys.

steps.action: The British-American composer David Bruce has wr
steps.text: The British-American composer David Bruce has written a clarinet quintet entitled "Gumboots", which was inspired by Gumboot dancing.

steps.action: It was commissioned by Carnegie Hall in 2008 and
steps.text: It was commissioned by Carnegie Hall in 2008 and can be heard in full on their website, performed by Todd Palmer and the St.

steps.action: The group respects the origins of the dance and
steps.text: The group respects the origins of the dance and tries to be as authentic and educative as possible when performing.

steps.action: In South Africa these are more commonly called g
steps.text: In South Africa these are more commonly called gumboots.

steps.action: This sound would be a code or a different callin
steps.text: This sound would be a code or a different calling to say something to another person a short distance away.

steps.action: This was used to communicate in the mines as the
steps.text: This was used to communicate in the mines as there was strictly no talking otherwise there would be severe, drastic punishments at the discretion of their superior.

steps.action: The mines were very noisy workplaces, with pneum
steps.text: The mines were very noisy workplaces, with pneumatic drills at work most of the time; in those days (until the mid 1970s) ear-defenders did not exist in South African mines.

steps.action: At the beginning of every year, around the month
steps.text: At the beginning of every year, around the months of March and April, the team is responsible for choreographing and performing at the One World festival, an annual concert in Victoria, BC.
